title: Integration of Whole Genome Sequencing in National Salmonella Surveillance
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     a     leading     cause     of     foodborne     illness     worldwide  ,   creating     significant     public     health     and     economic     burdens  .   Traditional     serotyping     and     culture-based     methods     often     provide     delayed     results     and     limited     resolution     for     outbreak     source     tracing  .   Whole     Genome     Sequencing   (WGS)   offers     a     transformative     approach  ,   enabling     high-resolution     characterization     of     Salmonella     isolates  ,   identification     of     antimicrobial     resistance     genes  ,   and   real-  time     tracking     of     outbreak     strains  .   Integrating   WGS   into     national     surveillance     systems     can     strengthen     early     detection  ,   enhance     outbreak     response  ,   and     inform     targeted     control     measures     across     the     food     chain  .   This     policy     brief     advocates     for     building   WGS   capacity  ,   developing     standardized     protocols  ,   and     fostering   data-  sharing     networks     to     improve     national     food     safety     governance     and     protect     public     health     from     Salmonella  -related     foodborne     disease  .
